Client 1: No, it’s really just the mortgage.

Advisor: Hi Mary, hi Jame—nice to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into numbers, what would make this meeting a win for you?
Client 1: Thanks. Honestly, a plan that doesn’t make our month-to-month feel tight—like, clear priorities and what we should do next.
Client 2: Yeah, same. Simple is good. I get lost when it’s too many moving parts.
Advisor: Totally fair. Mary, why don’t you start—what’s the big picture of income coming into the household right now?

Client 2: Two.
Advisor: Two dependents—thank you. And Mary, when you think about taxes, what bracket do you usually land in—ballpark is fine.
Client 1: We’re in the 35% bracket band.
Advisor: Got it—thanks. Let me note that down.
Advisor: Now I want to do that inventory we mentioned—starting with income. Mary, you’ve got your main job income, and then is there a bonus component too?

Client 1: Still true—nothing active.
Advisor: Okay—thanks, that’s the full inventory. Before we shift into recommendations, I want to confirm a couple household basics with you both.
Advisor: Mary, Jame—are you two married?
Client 1: Yes, we’re married.
Advisor: Got it. And just to confirm the household headcount—how many adults are in the household?

Advisor: Hi Mary, hi Jame—nice to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into numbers, what would make this meeting a win for you?
Client 1: Thanks. Honestly, a plan that doesn’t make our month-to-month feel tight—like, clear priorities and what we should do next.
Client 2: Yeah, same. Simple is good. I get lost when it’s too many moving parts.
Advisor: Totally fair. Mary, why don’t you start—what’s the big picture of income coming into the household right now?
Client 1: Combined, our household gross income is 99650 a year.

Advisor: Got it. And just to confirm the household headcount—how many adults are in the household?
Client 2: Two adults—just Mary and me.
Advisor: Perfect. And then, just so you know how I work—are you looking for this to be under an RIA relationship, meaning a registered investment advisor, versus like a brokerage-only setup?
Client 1: Yes—RIA (registered investment advisor).
Advisor: Great, thank you. Now, zooming out a bit—when you think about investing style, where do you land on the cautious-to-aggressive spectrum?

Advisor: Great, thank you. Now, zooming out a bit—when you think about investing style, where do you land on the cautious-to-aggressive spectrum?
Client 2: Uh, probably more conservative? Like we don’t want anything too wild.
Client 1: I mean—growth. We’re growth, but with some guardrails because the monthly stuff is tight.
Advisor: That makes sense—growth overall, with a plan so cash flow doesn’t get surprised.
Advisor: Let me just do a quick wrap so I’m not missing anything: income-wise, Mary you’ve got the monthly bonus line at 23200 annually, and Jame you’ve got two Social Security lines—35350 annually and 41150 annually. Still right?

Client 2: I’m retired.
Advisor: Okay. When you say retired, is there any wages or salary coming in for you at all, or should I treat your earned income as zero and just focus on the other income streams we listed?
Client 2: No wages. For gross annual income on my side, it’s 28550.
Advisor: Thanks—hang on, I’m just writing that down. And just to be clear, that 28550 is your gross annual income number we’re using for you, separate from those two Social Security lines we already listed earlier?
Client 2: Yeah, separate. The Social Security ones were 35350 and 41150 annually, but my gross annual income is 28550.

Advisor: Perfect. Next step, I’ll map a growth-tilted plan that still keeps that buffer you care about, Mary, and we’ll keep it simple enough that it doesn’t feel like a second job, Jame.
Advisor: Okay—before I start sketching options, I want to make sure I’ve got your basics right for you, Jame, since we moved pretty fast through the inventory.

Client 2: Yep—spouse/partner.
Advisor: Got it. And your current work situation—are you working, between jobs, or retired?

Client 2: Yeah, Social Security. There are two lines for me—one is 35350 annually and the other is 41150 annually.
Advisor: Okay, that helps—two separate Social Security income streams for you.
